title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of warm Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a healthy and flavorful Middle Eastern staple.

description: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a beloved Middle Eastern comfort food, cherished for its nourishing qualities and vibrant flavors. This hearty, plant-based soup combines red lentils with aromatic spices, fresh vegetables, and a bright squeeze of lemon, creating a truly satisfying meal. It's a simple yet elegant dish that brings warmth and tradition to any table.

instructions.title: Prepare and Rinse Lentils
instructions.text: Thoroughly rinse the red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain well and set aside. This removes any debris and helps prevent cloudiness.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic, diced carrot, and diced celery, cooking for another 3-5 minutes until vegetables begin to soften.

instructions.title: Toast Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the cumin powder and turmeric powder. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant. This helps to deepen the flavor of the spices.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, then pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend (Optional) and Season
instructions.text: For a creamier texture, use an immersion blender to blend a portion of the soup directly in the pot, or carefully transfer about half of the soup to a regular blender (blend in batches if needed) and return it to the pot. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Garnish each serving with a sprinkle of fresh chopped cilantro or parsley. Serve immediately with extra lemon wedges on the side.

tips: For an extra layer of authentic flavor, consider adding a pinch of dried mint during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Adjust the consistency of the soup by adding more hot water or broth if you prefer a thinner soup after blending.
Adas tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
article:
Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is more than just a dish; it's a warm embrace in a bowl, a staple in Middle Eastern households, and a testament to the power of simple ingredients. This hearty, nutritious, and incredibly flavorful soup holds a special place in Lebanese cuisine, cherished for its comforting qualities and ease of preparation. Whether you're seeking a wholesome meal on a chilly evening, a nutritious lunch, or an authentic taste of the Levant, Adas delivers every time. Its vibrant color, silky texture, and zesty finish make it an irresistible choice for food lovers everywhere, including those exploring vegan and vegetarian options.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas?

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, or Adas (pronounced "ah-das"), is a traditional Middle Eastern soup made primarily from red lentils. Unlike some other lentil soups that might feature a medley of vegetables and spices, Adas often prides itself on its simplicity, allowing the earthy flavor of the lentils to shine through, beautifully complemented by aromatic ingredients like onions, garlic, and fresh lemon juice. This soup is a cornerstone of Lebanese home cooking, passed down through generations. It's renowned for being incredibly nourishing, packed with protein and fiber, making it a complete and satisfying meal. Historically, lentils have been a vital food source in the Middle East due to their affordability, availability, and nutritional value, making Adas a beloved and accessible dish for all. Its naturally vegan and gluten-free properties also make it a fantastic option for a wide range of dietary preferences.

## Ingredients You Need for Adas

Crafting a delicious Lebanese Red Lentil Soup requires a few key, humble ingredients that, when combined, create magic. The beauty lies in their freshness and quality.

*   **Red Lentils:** Approximately 1 cup (200g) of dried red lentils. Ensure they are split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily to create the soup's signature creamy texture.
*   **Onion:** 1 large yellow onion, finely chopped. This forms the aromatic base.
*   **Garlic:** 3-4 cloves, minced. Garlic adds depth and a pungent warmth.
*   **Olive Oil:** 2-3 tablespoons of good quality extra virgin olive oil for sautéing and drizzling.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** 6-8 cups (1.5-2 liters). Using vegetable broth can add an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Lemon Juice:** 1/4 to 1/2 cup of freshly squeezed lemon juice. This is crucial for the soup's characteristic tang and brightness.
*   **Salt:** To taste.
*   **Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Cumin:** 1-2 teaspoons ground cumin. Cumin is the quintessential spice for lentil dishes, lending an earthy, warm aroma.
*   **Dried Mint (optional but recommended):** 1-2 teaspoons dried mint, crushed. This offers a unique, refreshing note that elevates the soup.
*   **Fresh Mint (for garnish):** A handful of fresh mint leaves, chopped, for serving.

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

Making Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is straightforward and rewarding. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl every time:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any dust or debris. Set aside to drain.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Add Lentils and Spices:** Stir in the rinsed lentils, ground cumin, and dried mint (if using). Sauté for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and enhance their flavor.
4.  **Simmer the Soup:** Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend (Optional):** For a smoother consistency, you can use an immersion blender to blend a portion or all of the soup directly in the pot. Alternatively, carefully transfer portions to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.
6.  **Season and Finish:** Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper to taste. The amount of lemon juice can be adjusted to your preference – a generous squeeze is traditional.
7.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and a sprinkle of fresh chopped mint.

## Pro Tips for the Best Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

Achieving the perfect bowl of Adas involves more than just following a recipe. Here are some pro tips to elevate your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup:

*   **Don't Skip the Rinse:** Rinsing lentils is crucial. It not only removes impurities but can also help prevent digestive discomfort.
*   **Quality Olive Oil:** Since olive oil is a prominent flavor, especially as a garnish, invest in a good quality extra virgin olive oil. Its fruity, peppery notes will significantly enhance the soup.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Bottled lemon juice simply won't cut it. Freshly squeezed lemon juice provides a brighter, more complex acidity that is essential to the authentic taste of Adas. Add it at the very end to preserve its fresh flavor.
*   **Toast Your Spices:** Briefly toasting the cumin and dried mint with the aromatics before adding liquid helps to bloom their flavors, making them more potent and aromatic.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** If your soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. If it's too thin, let it simmer uncovered for a few more minutes to reduce.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice as needed. The balance of these three is critical for a truly delicious Adas.
*   **The Power of Dried Mint:** While optional, dried mint adds a subtle, refreshing, and distinctly Middle Eastern flavor that truly sets Adas apart. Don't confuse it with fresh mint for garnish; they play different roles.

## Serving Suggestions for Adas

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is wonderfully versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Here are some traditional and popular serving suggestions:

*   **Fresh Lemon Wedges:** Always serve Adas with extra lemon wedges on the side, allowing each diner to add more zest to their individual bowl.
*   **Warm Pita Bread:** Tear off pieces of warm pita bread to scoop up the soup. It's the perfect vehicle for soaking up all the delicious liquid.
*   **Fresh Vegetables:** A simple side salad with crisp lettuce, cucumbers, and tomatoes dressed in a lemon-tahini dressing or a simple vinaigrette provides a refreshing contrast.
*   **Pickles:** A small plate of mixed Lebanese pickles (such as pickled cucumbers, turnips, or wild cucumbers) offers a delightful sour crunch that complements the soup beautifully.
*   **Green Onions:** Chopped green onions can be sprinkled over the top for a mild oniony bite and a pop of color.
*   **Chili Flakes:** For those who enjoy a bit of heat, a pinch of red chili flakes or a drizzle of chili oil can be added.
*   **Garlic Bread:** While not strictly traditional, a slice of garlic bread can also be a delicious accompaniment.

## Variations and Substitutions for Red Lentil Soup

While the classic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, it's also adaptable to personal preferences and ingredients on hand.

*   **Add Vegetables:** For a heartier soup, consider adding finely diced carrots or celery along with the onions. Spinach or Swiss chard can also be stirred in during the last few minutes of cooking until wilted.
*   **Spice it Up:** For a more complex spice profile, a pinch of turmeric can be added for color and earthy notes, or a dash of cayenne pepper for heat. Some recipes might include a touch of coriander.
*   **Different Herbs:** While dried mint is traditional, fresh parsley or cilantro can be stirred in at the end for a different fresh herb flavor.
*   **Smoked Paprika:** A teaspoon of smoked paprika can add a lovely smoky depth to the soup, though it deviates slightly from the traditional Lebanese profile.
*   **Tomato Paste:** A tablespoon of tomato paste can be added with the aromatics to give the soup a richer color and a subtle tangy sweetness.
*   **Vegetable Broth:** While water is commonly used, vegetable broth will undoubtedly add more depth and umami to the soup.
*   **Vegan "Meat" Crumbles:** For those who want more texture, vegan ground "meat" crumbles can be browned with the onions and garlic.
*   **Toasted Vermicelli:** Some versions of Middle Eastern lentil soup include toasted vermicelli pasta. Toast a handful of broken vermicelli in a separate pan with a little olive oil until golden, then add it to the soup during the last 10 minutes of simmering.

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is more than just a meal; it's a comforting ritual, a taste of home, and a truly satisfying culinary experience. Its simplicity belies its profound flavor and incredible nutritional benefits. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to fall in love with Adas – a timeless classic that brings warmth and joy to every spoonful.
